title: Avocado Toast with Boiled Eggs (High-Protein Breakfast) | Eati
introduction: Avocado toast with boiled eggs is a 345-calorie, 22g protein breakfast that combines healthy fats, slow-release carbs, and complete protein in one 5-minute plate. Boiling your eggs ahead of time makes this one of the fastest weekday breakfasts — and boxed lunch — you can keep on rotation.

sections.heading: Ingredients
sections.content:
- 1 slice whole-grain bread (about 40g, ~100 cal)
- 1/2 medium avocado (70g)
- 2 large boiled eggs
- 1/2 tsp lemon juice
- Pinch of red pepper flakes
- Pinch of sea salt and black pepper
- Optional: 1 tsp everything bagel seasoning, microgreens, cherry tomatoes

sections.heading: Instructions
sections.content:
1. Toast the whole-grain bread until golden.
2. While toasting, mash 1/2 avocado in a bowl with lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
3. Slice boiled eggs lengthwise or into rounds.
4. Spread mashed avocado on the toast.
5. Arrange egg slices on top. Finish with red pepper flakes and optional seasoning.
6. Log the portion in Eati to confirm your macros.

sections.heading: Calories, Fat, and Protein (Estimated)
sections.content:
Calories: 345 kcal per serving
Fat: 17g (mostly heart-healthy monounsaturated)
Protein: 22g
Carbs: 25g (7g fiber)

Swap to low-carb bread to drop to ~275 cal while keeping 22g protein and 6g+ fiber.

sections.heading: Why Avocado + Eggs Is a Balanced Fat-Loss Breakfast
sections.content:
Despite its instagram reputation, avocado toast can be genuinely useful for fat loss when built correctly. Here's what the macros prove:

Balanced breakdown:
• 345 calories
• 22g protein (high)
• 17g fat (mostly MUFAs from avocado)
• 25g carbs with 7g fiber
• Very filling thanks to fat + protein + fiber

Avocado's satiety edge:
Avocado is one of the few fats with strong satiety per calorie. A 2022 study in the Journal of Nutrition found that adding avocado to a breakfast reduced afternoon hunger by 40% compared to a similar-calorie meal without avocado. The soluble fiber and monounsaturated fats slow gastric emptying.

Eggs supply complete protein:
Two large eggs deliver 12g of high-quality protein with all 9 essential amino acids. Combined with the ~7g protein from whole-grain bread, you reach 22g — the effective minimum for muscle protein synthesis.

Why whole-grain bread matters:
• 100 cal per slice vs white bread's 75 cal (but 3× the fiber).
• Lower glycemic response — no blood sugar spike.
• 3–4g of protein per slice.
• B vitamins and minerals that white bread loses.

How it compares:
• Plain avocado toast (no eggs): 230 cal, 7g protein.
• Bagel + cream cheese: 400 cal, 13g protein.
• This recipe: 345 cal, 22g protein, 7g fiber.

For the full breakfast cluster, see [20 high-protein low-calorie breakfast ideas](/blog/20-high-protein-low-calorie-breakfast-ideas). For more protein-forward food ideas, see [high-protein low-calorie foods](/blog/high-protein-low-calorie-foods).

sections.heading: How to Boil Eggs Perfectly (Every Time)
sections.content:
Perfect boiled eggs are one of those kitchen skills worth 10 minutes to master. Here's how:

The best method (shell slides off):
1. Place eggs in a saucepan, cover with 1 inch of cold water.
2. Bring to a rolling boil.
3. Remove from heat, cover with lid.
4. Set timer:
   • 6 minutes: soft-boiled, jammy yolk (perfect for avocado toast).
   • 8 minutes: medium-boiled, soft but set yolk.
   • 10 minutes: hard-boiled, fully set.
   • 12 minutes: fully cooked, ideal for meal prep.
5. Transfer to ice water bath for 5 minutes.
6. Tap, roll, peel.

Key tips that actually work:
• Use week-old eggs — fresh eggs are notoriously hard to peel.
• Add 1 tsp baking soda to the water — raises pH and makes peeling easier.
• Ice bath is non-negotiable — it stops cooking and shrinks the egg from the shell.
• Peel under running water for the smoothest results.

Alternative methods:
• Instant Pot: 5-5-5 method (5 min cook, 5 min natural release, 5 min ice bath).
• Air fryer: 275°F for 15–17 min (no water needed).
• Steaming: 12 min for hard-boiled. Easiest peeling of all methods.

Meal prep guidance:
• Boil 8–12 eggs Sunday.
• Store in shell: 7 days in fridge.
• Store peeled: 5 days (in water-covered container, change daily).
• Label with cook date.

How many eggs per day is safe?
Current research (2020 BMJ meta-analysis) shows that for most healthy adults, 1–3 whole eggs per day does not increase cardiovascular risk. The recipe's 2 eggs is well within this range.

For more egg recipes, see [egg muffins meal prep recipe](/blog/egg-muffins-meal-prep-recipe) and [scrambled eggs with mushrooms](/blog/scrambled-eggs-mushrooms-recipe).

sections.heading: 7 Avocado Toast Variations That Add More Protein
sections.content:
The base recipe delivers 22g protein. These variations push higher or swap flavor profiles:

1. Smoked salmon (380 cal, 28g protein):
• Add 2 oz smoked salmon + dill + capers. Inspired by [smoked salmon & cream cheese toast](/blog/smoked-salmon-cream-cheese-toast-recipe).

2. Turkey bacon (370 cal, 26g protein):
• Add 2 slices turkey bacon + tomato slice. BLT-style without the mayo.

3. Cottage cheese smash (330 cal, 28g protein):
• Replace 1/2 the avocado with 1/4 cup cottage cheese. Higher protein, lower fat.

4. Everything bagel (355 cal, 23g protein):
• Base + 1 tsp everything seasoning + radish slices.

5. Chicken & avocado (420 cal, 34g protein):
• Add 2 oz shredded cooked chicken. Lunch-worthy breakfast. Similar to our [chicken & avocado breakfast bowl](/blog/chicken-avocado-breakfast-bowl-recipe).

6. Mediterranean (340 cal, 23g protein):
• Add crumbled feta + olive halves + cherry tomatoes + oregano.

7. Mexican (355 cal, 22g protein):
• Mash avocado with lime + cilantro. Top with salsa + cotija cheese + pepper flakes.

Bread alternatives (similar or better macros):
• Ezekiel sprouted bread (100 cal, 4g protein): higher protein.
• Dave's Killer Bread Protein (110 cal, 5g protein): highest protein bread at grocery stores.
• Low-carb bread (50 cal, 5g protein): drops total to ~275 cal.
• Rye crispbread (35 cal, 2g protein): crunchy base, lower calorie.

Avocado hack:
Use 1/4 avocado instead of 1/2 to save 80 cal. You barely notice the difference, and the flavor profile stays intact. Great for portion control on aggressive fat loss.

sections.heading: Make It a Full Meal (And Prep It for the Week)
sections.content:
Avocado toast doesn't have to be a grab-and-go breakfast. Scale it up or prep smartly:

Complete 500-calorie breakfast:
• Base avocado toast (345 cal).
• Add 1/2 cup berries (35 cal).
• Add 1 cup black coffee with splash of milk (15 cal).
• Total: 395 cal, 23g protein, great start to the day.

When you want to meal prep:
• Boil 8 eggs Sunday (stores 7 days in shell).
• Pre-slice whole-grain bread and keep in the freezer (toasts from frozen in 2 min).
• Avocado does NOT meal prep well — it browns quickly. Slice fresh each morning.

Avocado ripeness hack:
• Bought too firm? Leave on counter 2–3 days.
• Bought too soft? Mash with lemon and freeze in silicone cubes (lasts 1 month).
• The sweet spot: gives slightly under firm pressure but doesn't squish.

How to tell a ripe avocado:
• Remove the small stem cap — if it's green underneath, it's ripe. Brown = overripe.
• Gentle squeeze test: should give slightly, not squish.
• Skin: Hass avocados turn nearly black when ripe.

Budget tip:
• 1 avocado = 2–3 servings. Slice half, mash half, cover with lemon juice + plastic wrap pressed against surface — keeps 24 hrs without browning.
• Frozen avocado cubes (already ripe): $4 per bag, 8 servings. Great backup.

Travel and work-friendly:
• Pack mashed avocado in a small container, boiled eggs whole, and bread separately.
• Assemble at destination in 60 seconds.

For more efficient breakfast planning, see [how to track calories with an app](/blog/how-to-track-calories-with-an-app).

midArticleCta: Hit 22g of protein and healthy fats before 9 AM. Log this avocado-egg toast in Eati and see how it fits your daily calorie plan.
conclusion: A 5-minute, 345-calorie breakfast with 22g protein, 7g fiber, and healthy monounsaturated fats. Keep whole-grain bread, avocados, and a stash of boiled eggs on hand and you always have a grown-up breakfast ready to go.
metaTitle: Avocado Toast with Boiled Eggs: 22g Protein, 345 Calories (Easy Breakfast)
metaDescription: Healthy avocado toast with boiled eggs — 22g protein in 345 calories. Includes 7 variations, perfect boiled-egg technique, and avocado meal prep tips for the week.

faqs.question: How many calories in avocado toast with boiled eggs?
faqs.answer: About 345 calories per serving with 1 slice whole-grain bread, 1/2 avocado, and 2 boiled eggs. Delivers 22g protein, 17g healthy fats, and 7g fiber. Swapping to low-carb bread drops it to ~275 cal with similar protein.

faqs.question: Is avocado toast with eggs healthy for weight loss?
faqs.answer: Yes, when portions are controlled. The fiber-protein-fat combo keeps you full 4+ hours, and 345 calories leaves plenty of budget for lunch and dinner. Watch the avocado portion — use 1/2 avocado max (70g, 120 cal) to avoid creeping calories.

faqs.question: How long should I boil eggs for avocado toast?
faqs.answer: 6–7 minutes gives you a jammy, creamy yolk that pairs beautifully with avocado. For fully set yolks, boil 10 minutes. Use the ice bath method afterwards for clean peeling. Week-old eggs peel more easily than fresh ones.

faqs.question: What bread is best for avocado toast?
faqs.answer: Whole-grain or sprouted-grain bread (Ezekiel, Dave's Killer Bread Protein) delivers the best fiber-protein combo with 3–5g protein per slice. For lower calories, use low-carb bread (50 cal, 5g protein) or rye crispbread.

faqs.question: Can I meal prep avocado toast?
faqs.answer: Partially. Boil 8 eggs on Sunday and pre-slice bread into the freezer. But avocado browns quickly — always slice fresh. If needed, mashed avocado with lemon juice stays green for 24 hours with plastic wrap pressed directly against the surface.
